$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `embedding_status` repo (Phase-2). One
committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-embedding-status-fixture.ts + embedding-status-tier2.ts)
and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness embedding_status tier-2 test).
What this repo banks: an all-TEXT marshaling surface — UUID columns (userId,
entityId, profileId) as TEXT, two enum columns as TEXT (entityType:
MEMORY/FILE/HELP_DOC/CONVERSATION_CHUNK/MOUNT_CHUNK; status:
PENDING/EMBEDDED/FAILED, default PENDING, set explicitly here), and two NULLABLE
TEXT columns (embeddedAt, error). No booleans, numbers, JSON, or BLOB.
⚠️ updatedAt IS MINTED, NOT PINNED. v4's EmbeddingStatusRepository OVERRIDES
the base create/update and sets updatedAt = getCurrentTimestamp() (live now)
UNCONDITIONALLY — options.updatedAt on create and any updatedAt in an update
patch are IGNORED. So create options carry only {id, createdAt}, update data
carries NO updatedAt, and the tier-2 harness placeholder-normalizes the
updatedAt column on BOTH sides (ids + createdAt + every other column stay
exact). createdAt IS honored on create (options.createdAt || now) and
preserved on update.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders / tags / tfidf_vocabulary fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday
pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: ids + createdAt + payload columns are pinned; only the minted
updatedAt column needs normalization (placeholdered both sides).

ops.comment: New PENDING status for a HELP_DOC entity; embeddedAt + error both null.
New EMBEDDED status for a CONVERSATION_CHUNK entity; embeddedAt set, error null.
Update seed row 1 (PENDING-like flip): set status EMBEDDED and embeddedAt. createdAt preserved; updatedAt minted (placeholdered).
Update the first created row: set status FAILED and an error message. updatedAt minted (placeholdered).
